content.full_text:
CommissioIL 
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
r<lchnical Assistance/Administration 
Executive f legal 
Enforcement 
Conflict of Interest 
(916) 322-5660 
Forest Greber 
Deputy County Counsel 
County of Kern 
1415 Truxtun Avenue 
Bakersfield, CA 
93301 
Dear Mr. Greber: 
322-5901 
322-6441 
January 4, 1978 
Re: 
Opinion Request No. 77-042 
This is in response to your letter to the Commis-
sion dated October 26, 1977, requesting the Commission to 
issue an opinion concerning the necessity of development and 
promulgation of Conflict of Interest Codes for the Kern 
322·6444 
County Emergency Medical Care Committee and the Kern County 
Committee on School District Organization. 
The Commission 
will not issue a formal opinion in this matter because no sub-
stantial question of interpretation of the Political Reform 
Act is raised. 
I trust, however, the following informal ad-
vice will enable you to resolve the matter. 
As code reviewing body, the Board of Supervisors 
of Kern County has the responsibility and authority for 
reviewing Conflict of Interest Codes developed by county 
agencies. 
However, as you indicate in your letter, Government 
Code Section 820l9(c)1/, read in conjunction with 2 Cal. Adm. 
Code Section l8700(a) (1), dictates a conclusion that only those 
agencies with "decision-making authority" be required to develop 
and promulgate Conflict of Interest Codes. 
The staff has reviewed the statutes in question and 
agrees with your tentative conclusion that, as currently struc-
tured, the boards or commissions in question do not appear 
"decision-making" within the meaning of l8700(a) (1). 
II 
All statutory references herein are to the Govern-
ment Code unless otherwise noted. 


ri"orest Greber 
Page Two 
January 
I 
1978 
In the case of school district reorganization commit-
tees, this conclusion is buttressed by the fact that certain 
designated employees in the State Department of Education, and 
the members of the State Board of Education, are required to dis-
close their interests in real property throughout the state which 
they know or have reason to know are located within an area sub-
ject to school district reorganization. 
This was required because 
those persons at the State level hold ultimate decision-making 
authority concerning such reorganizations. 
With respect to the Emergency Medical Care Committee, 
it appears that it has no "decision-making authority" in its 
I 
functions as they relate to areawide health planning agencies,~1 
the Advisory Health Council, the Department of Health or the 
Board of Supervisors and, therefore, should not be required to 
develop a conflict of interest code. 
Absent changed circumstances or additional information, 
we believe that the agencies in question need not be required 
to develop Conflict of' Interest Codes by the Board of Supervisors 
at this time. 
If you have any further questions concerning this 
or any matter, please feel free to calIon Ken Goshorn of the 
Conflicts of Interest Division at (916) 322-6444. 
MB:KWG: jo 
Sincerely, 
r f:)\.\.1 
) lJ. _ ,)(.',,-- -fr,-
Hichael Bennett 
Executive Director 
~I We have not, at this time I required area'tvide compre-
hensive health planning agencies to develop Conflict of Interest 
Codes. 
This decision was made because those agencies have per-
formed an advisory function. 
At this time, because of legisla-
tive changes in the enabling acts for areawide comprehensive 
health planning agencies, we are reviewing our approach to these 
agencies. 
However, none of those legislative changes affect the 
relationship of emergency medical care cOID~ittees to the areawide 
health planning agencies.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether Kern County must require the Emergency Medical Care Committee and the Committee on School District Organization to develop and promulgate Conflict of Interest Codes under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The two committees do not have 'decision-making authority' as defined by Government Code Section 18700(a)(1) and therefore are not required to develop Conflict of Interest Codes. The FPPC staff agrees with the county's conclusion that these agencies need not be required to develop such codes absent changed circumstances.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a formal FPPC response letter dated January 4, 1978. Explicitly states no formal opinion will be issued but provides informal advice. Contains verbatim Question and Conclusion present implicitly throughout body of letter rather than in labeled sections.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether Kern County must require the Emergency Medical Care Committee and the Committee on School District Organization to develop and promulgate Conflict of Interest Codes under the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: The two committees do not have 'decision-making authority' as defined by Government Code Section 18700(a)(1) and therefore are not required to develop Conflict of Interest Codes. The FPPC staff agrees with the county's conclusion that these agencies need not be required to develop such codes absent changed circumstances.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines to issue a formal opinion but provides informal advice that Kern County's Emergency Medical Care Committee and Committee on School District Organization need not develop Conflict of Interest Codes because neither body possesses the requisite 'decision-making authority' under the Political Reform Act.
